title: festuca ovina subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. ochroleuca, also known as sheep's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and its anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. ochroleuca include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
